dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to 320 Marker Street for a medical alarm.
Hi, I'm Taylor, an EMT with the rescue squad. Can you tell me what made you call 911 today?
Yeah… I've got chest and back pain, started about an hour ago from when I thought something in my bed slipped.
Just checking in, Mr. Smith-how are you doing right now?
I'm fine, just hurting badly in my chest and back.
I get that-what is your full name and your birth year?
Robert Smith… my birthday is June 20th, I was born in 1953.
Right. What year is it today, Mr. Smith?
It's 2019. What city and state are we in?
Correct: [CITY], [STATE]. One more check-can you follow my finger with your eyes?
Sure can.
Your airway looks clear and patent. Do you have any trouble breathing?
No, I'm breathing fine.
Chest movement is even. Any shortness of breath or unusual sensation in your chest?
Some pressure, but sharp pain radiates into my back-it's a 9 out of 10.
When exactly did it start and what were you doing then?
I was cooking and I thought the bed shifted. I didn't fall, just felt a sudden jolt of pain.
Any numbness or weakness in your arms or legs?
No-not that I noticed.
Pulse is 102 and strong. Does the chest pain stay the same when you breathe?
Respirations are steady and normal at 20 per minute.
Blood pressure is 146 over 86. Pulse is strong.
Oxygen saturation is at 97%.
Blood glucose is not detectable on current reading.
We'll place ECG pads now for heart rhythm. It won't hurt you.

ECG monitor shows sinus rhythm. Have you had a history of heart issues? 27. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:signs_symptoms; EMT: What were you doing when you first noticed the pain? 28. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:signs_symptoms; Patient: I was cooking. The bed moved and I felt a sudden jolt into my back and chest. No fall-just minor movement.

Tell me about your medical history-any previous problems or conditions? 30. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.:pertinent_past_medical_history; Patient: Yes-I had blood clots, so I'm on Coumadin. I also have a history of cardiac arrhythmias, like bradycardia.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate the chest and back pain?
9 in both. Sharp and constant-which makes it hard to move.
Does the pain spread to your arms, jaw, or neck?
No. It's right here-between the shoulder blades and into my sternum.
Let me inspect your head and neck. Any pain or discomfort?
No. I had no head injury.
I'll listen to your lungs. Take a deep breath in.
(deep breath)
Lungs are clear. Do you have asthma or COPD?
No asthma. I do smoke but not on a daily basis.
Skin is warm, pink, and dry. Any rash, bruising, or swelling?
No-no marks or bruises. Just back and chest pain.
Peripheral pulses are strong and regular. Any numbness or pain in limbs?
No numbness but I can't twist or bend easily.
Let's do a 12-lead EKG to check your heart rhythm.
EKG results showed sinus tach with an incomplete right bundle branch block and left anterior fascicular block.
Per EKG and your symptoms, we'll follow protocol 3-10 for general pain control.
Now your pulse is 100-checking blood pressure again.
Blood pressure is 0 over 0. I'll repeat this in a few minutes.
SpO2 is at 95% and still within a normal range.
We'll recheck your EKG soon for any rhythm changes.
Pulse now 104, blood pressure 141 over 85. SpO2 is at 95% now.
That's normal-for now. Oxygen levels are good.
Pulse is at 101, blood pressure is 0 over 0 again. SpO2 has improved to 96%.
Pressure is now 130 over 83, pulse is 102. SpO2 is up to 97%.
Blood pressure is back to 0 over 0. Pulse is 103, SpO2 is 98% now.
Pulse back to 100, blood pressure is at 136 over 80. SpO2 remains at 96%.
We're headed to [COMPANY_NAME] Cold.
Let's get you secured. Please lie still while I attach the harness.
Got you set on the stretcher-now on the move with the harness fastened.
All good. Harness is secure. You're on the stretcher now, Mr. Smith.
That's okay-just please don't jostle too much for the back pain.
Patient is being taken to the ED at [COMPANY_NAME] Cold-Robert Smith, 69, with back and chest pain.
EKG showed sinus tach with incomplete right bundle block and left anterior fascicular block.
Under protocol 3-10 with EKG monitoring throughout transport.
Receiving now. Please be gentle for the back pain.
Ready for sheet lift. Seat belt is in place for transfer safety.
Transfer complete. Handover successful.
Feel free to reach out if anything comes up.
Transfer to hospital complete. Ready to return to base now.
Yep, documents already in file at base.
Back in the car, let's debrief on the way home.
